perturbation.original_text: PC Financial shall pay to Mobimagic a fixed service fee in the amount of 970,000 United States Dollars (USD 970,000) for each month (the "Fee") within the timeline as permitted under the prevailing India regulations. It is stipulated in this Agreement that Mobimagic will send an invoice to PC Financial on monthly basis according to the progress of the service. After PC Financial confirms that invoice contents are correct, PC Financial shall pay within 45 days after confirming thus.
perturbation.changed_text: PC Financial shall pay to Mobimagic a fixed service fee in the amount of 970,000 United States Dollars (USD 970,000) for each month (the "Fee") within the timeline as permitted under the prevailing India regulations. It is stipulated in this Agreement that Mobimagic will send an invoice to PC Financial on monthly basis according to the progress of the service. After PC Financial confirms that invoice contents are correct, PC Financial shall pay within 60 days after confirming thus.
perturbation.explanation: The payment term has been changed from '45 days' to '60 days'. This creates a contradiction with section 6.5, which states that PC Financial shall raise any problems regarding the invoice within '20 business days', effectively implying a shorter payment review window than the extended 60-day payment period now provided in section 6.1. If they have 60 days to pay the invoice, that means there is a contradiction between the period in which the invoices can be raised and when it needs to be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case of any complaint about using the Services as set out in this Agreement PC Financial shall raise the problems within 20 business days after getting Mobimaigc's invoice. Both Parties shall engage in reasonable efforts to negotiate and solve the problems together. If the cause of the difference remains undetermined after the aforesaid efforts of the Parties, an independent third party, (e.g., external auditors) may be employed to give an inspection. The costs arising out of the third- party inspection shall be borne by the Parties according to their respective faults.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the payment term from 45 to 60 days. The contradicted text states that PC Financial must raise any problems about the invoice within 20 business days. This creates a direct contradiction. If PC Financial has 60 days to pay, but only 20 days to raise issues, there's an inconsistency in the time allocated for invoice review versus the payment period.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include information which (a) is known by the receiving Party, free of any obligation to keep it confidential; (b) is at the time of disclosure, or thereafter becomes, publicly available through no wrongful act of the receiving Party; (c) Is independently developed by the receiving Party, without relying on or referring to the Confidential Information of disclosing Party; or (d) is approved for release by prior written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information does not include information which (a) is known by the receiving Party, free of any obligation to keep it confidential; (b) is at the time of disclosure, or thereafter becomes, publicly available through no wrongful act of the receiving Party; (c) Is independently developed by the receiving Party, without relying on or referring to the Confidential Information of disclosing Party; (d) is approved for release by prior written authorization of the disclosing Party; or (e) becomes known to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party that is not subject to a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'e' creates a contradiction. While section 7 mandates a 5-year confidentiality period, this addition implies information loses confidentiality immediately upon being known from another source, regardless of the 5-year period. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty on when the obligation to keep the information persists or if an receiving party can use information after 5 years if known by other party after the disclosing part.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Party shall disclose the other Party's Confidential Information to any third party or use Confidential Information for any purpose other than fur the proper fulfillment of this Agreement. Each Party undertakes to safeguard the Confidential Information of the other Party with the same degree of care as it would apply to Its own Confidential Information and, in any case, with no less than reasonable care. Such obligations will survive the expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.justification: The addition of clause (e) to the definition of 'Confidential Information' directly contradicts the clause that states confidentiality obligations survive the agreement for five years. Clause (e) allows the receiving party to disclose information if it becomes known from another source, regardless of the five-year confidentiality period stipulated in the contradicted text. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations, making it unclear when the receiving party is permitted to disclose the information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either Party prior to the end of the Service Period if the other Party is in material breach of any tem, or condition of this Agreement and such breach la not remedied for a period of thirty (30) days after the Party in breach has been notified In writing of such breach by the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either Party prior to the end of the Service Period if the other Party is in material breach of any term, or condition of this Agreement and such breach la not remedied for a period of fifteen (15) days after the Party in breach has been notified In writing of such breach by the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The cure period for a material breach has been shortened from 30 days to 15 days. This creates a contradiction with Section 6.5, where disputes about the Services must be raised within 20 business days. By shortening the cure period to 15 days, it may be impossible for a party to fully remedy the breach as a result of those Services within the required time frame, as it might be raised later than when the breah needs to be resolved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case of any complaint about using the Services as set out in this Agreement PC Financial shall raise the problems within 20 business days after getting Mobimaigc's invoice. Both Parties shall engage in reasonable efforts to negotiate and solve the problems together. If the cause of the difference remains undetermined after the aforesaid efforts of the Parties, an independent third party, (e.g., external auditors) may be employed to give an inspection. The costs arising out of the third- party inspection shall be borne by the Parties according to their respective faults.
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the cure period for a material breach from 30 days to 15 days. The contradicted text requires that complaints about the services are raised within 20 business days after receiving the invoice. The explanation correctly states that shortening the cure period to 15 days might make it impossible for a party to fully remedy the breach within the timeframe that the complaint needs to be raised, creating an inconsistency regarding deadlines for resolution, as defined by the 'Inconsistencies' perturbation definition.
